  "findings": [
    "The tension in each sling leg is 6,000 lbs, which equals the tagged vertical WLL of 6,000 lbs.",
    "Rigging standards require a safety factor; the actual tension must not exceed the rated capacity of the sling at the specific angle of use."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Reject the current rigging plan.",
    "Increase the horizontal sling angle (e.g., to 45 or 60 degrees) or use slings with a higher Working Load Limit (WLL)."
  ],
  "rationale": "For a symmetric two-leg sling, the tension in each leg is calculated as (Load / 2) / sin(angle). Here, (6,000 lbs / 2) / sin(30 degrees) = 3,000 / 0.5 = 6,000 lbs. Since the tension in each leg (6,000 lbs) is equal to the rated vertical WLL (6,000 lbs), there is no safety margin for dynamic loading or angle-of-use reduction, violating safe rigging practices.",
  "references": [
    "ASME B30.9",
    "OSHA 1926.251"
  ]
}
